title: Dress Codes: Fashion, Law, and History
subtitle: 时尚法则如何塑造历史

paragraphs.en: It may sound surprising, but you don't have to be interested in fashion, or even in history, to enjoy Dress Codes: How the Laws of Fashion Made History. I happen to be interested in both, and ended up enjoying the book for completely different reasons. Richard Thompson Ford is a law professor, and you probably won't forget that for even one page.
paragraphs.zh: 这听起来可能令人惊讶，但您不必对时尚感兴趣，甚至不必对历史感兴趣，也能享受《着装规范：时尚法则如何塑造历史》这本书。我碰巧对两者都感兴趣，却出于截然不同的原因喜欢上了它。Richard Thompson Ford是一位法学教授，这一点在书中每一页都令人印象深刻。

words.meaning: n. 琐事；微不足道的事物

paragraphs.en: His carefully reasoned arguments, packed with examples, sound almost like reading a court opinion, only maybe wordier. You will probably never think of fashion as a trifle again.
paragraphs.zh: 他论证严密、例证丰富的文字读起来几乎像在阅读法庭意见书，只是更为详尽。读完此书，你大概再也不会认为时尚是一件无关紧要的事了。

paragraphs.en: Ford's thesis is that the best way to understand what particular fashions meant in any given era is to look at the restrictions placed on them. Through this lens, he shows us that the first laws passed in the 1200s to ensure that only the nobility were allowed to wear certain fabrics, colors and ornaments reflected the rise of the middle class, who were now able to imitate some of these fashions.
paragraphs.zh: 福特的核心论点是：理解某一时代特定时尚含义的最佳方式，就是审视加诸其上的种种限制。通过这一视角，他向我们展示了13世纪颁布的最早法律——规定只有贵族才能穿着特定面料、颜色和饰品——正是中产阶级崛起的折射，因为他们已有能力模仿这些时尚。

paragraphs.en: The status of the upper classes was threatened; fashion was a tool to preserve it. Ford takes the reader through the evolution of fashion while examining the underlying motivations of status, sex, power, and personality, which, he assumes, influenced all innovations in fashion in the past and which continue to influence us today. His writing is more than a little dense — dense with research, clauses, and precise adjectives and nouns.
paragraphs.zh: 上层阶级的地位受到了威胁，时尚成为维护这一地位的工具。福特带领读者梳理时尚的演变历程，同时深入剖析地位、性别、权力与个性等深层动机——他认为这些动机影响了过去所有的时尚创新，并持续影响着我们今天。他的文字相当厚重，充满研究成果、从句以及精准的形容词与名词。

paragraphs.en: But there's also humor and enough interesting episodes to make the writing appealing. No one is spared his sharp analysis: not the easy targets of 19th century women's crippling fashions nor the modern uniforms of Silicon Valley T-shirts. But the greatest strength of this book is its intellectual profoundness.
paragraphs.zh: 但书中也不乏幽默，以及足够多有趣的插曲，使文字极具吸引力。没有人能逃脱他犀利的分析：无论是19世纪女性那些摧残身体的时尚，还是硅谷T恤的现代"制服"，皆在其列。然而这本书（一本关于时尚的书！）最大的力量，在于其思想的深刻性。

paragraphs.en: Ford asks us to question unconscious beliefs, to realize that we almost never do so, to understand that the simplest choices are charged with meaning, and yet that meaning can and does change all the time. Consider the fact that a 1918 catalog insisted that boys and girls be dressed in the appropriate color. We believe our thinking today is evolved; Ford shows us it's not.
paragraphs.zh: 福特要求我们质疑那些无意识的信念，让我们意识到自己几乎从未这样做过，帮助我们理解最简单的选择都蕴含意义，而这种意义又时刻处于变化之中。想想这样一个事实：1918年的一本商品目录坚持要求男孩和女孩穿着"适当"的颜色。我们以为今天的思维已经进步了，福特却告诉我们并非如此。

words.meaning: adj. 无意识的；不自觉的
